pattern: Each day's best score only depends on where you ended the previous day, so the whole k-day journey is a small day-by-city dynamic program that tries staying versus arriving from every other city.
hints: The tourist can start in any city for free, so day 0's best score at city c is either staying there right away or having 'started' at some other city and immediately traveled into c.

For every later day, the best score ending at city c is the better of: continuing to stay in c from wherever yesterday's best score at c was, or traveling into c from whichever other city had the best score yesterday once you add that city's travel score into c.
Track one running best score per city across the k days -- at the very end the answer is simply the largest score among all cities on the final day.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, staying put beats traveling
statement example, a single travel move on day two
single city means only staying is possible every day
travel is always strictly better than staying every day
staying is always strictly better than any travel option

stress.description: random stay and travel score tables at moderate size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 60))
    k = max(1, min(size, 60))
    stay_score = [[rng.randint(1, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(k)]
    travel_score = [[0 if i == j else rng.randint(0, 100) for j in range(n)] for i in range(n)]
    return [n, k, stay_score, travel_score]
